mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth exceeds 2% quarterly and CPI inflation remains below 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects improving manufacturing and supply chain throughput, generating positive short-term market sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of months where industrial production increases, regardless of prior trend.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures directly correlate with increased corporate revenue expectations, prompting institutional buying that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth consistently fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55% of observations when payrolls grow by more than 100k and unemployment remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in housing starts signals collapsing construction demand and tightening credit conditions, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 60% of months following a housing starts decline exceeding 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing industrial production and persistent GDP growth indicates supply-side bottlenecks that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The supply-demand divergence signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production growth falls below 0.5% while GDP growth exceeds 2.5%.
